response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his career centers on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Ember) rather than the deep, current C++ expertise this role demands. His only professional C++ work was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list. He also lacks the industrial-automation context: no MQTT, no IEC 62443 or SDL exposure, no message-broker or OT-protocol experience. His DLang low-latency systems work shows real concurrency and performance chops, but it is not a substitute for 8-12 years of hands-on C++ in secure industrial environments.
strengths: Genuine low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second, showing strong concurrency fundamentals
Broad, sustained engineering career across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with a visible commitment to testing, TDD, and craftsmanship
Some direct C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI components, C++ audio thesis) plus IoT-adjacent GIS platform work
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and a decade old; current stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, not C++/STL
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, MQTT, or OT protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.)
No demonstrated experience architecting message-broker-based, networked multi-threaded C++ systems or mentoring teams on secure C++ standards

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 security background
Strong concurrency and low-latency systems skills (DLang)
